title: Spicy Ethiopian Cheese Wrap
description: A spicy, cheese-pull Ethiopian dish that is kid-friendly and features cream of tartar, beurre-monte, and is raw-friendly.
instructions: Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the skillet and swirl to coat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the berbere spice blend and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the crumbly cottage cheese and cook, stirring occasionally, until melted and smooth.
Add the softened butter and stir until combined.
Meanwhile, prepare the beurre-monte by whipping 1 cup of heavy cream until stiff peaks form.
Add 1 tablespoon of cream of tartar to the whipped cream and whip until stiff peaks form again.
To assemble the wraps, place a large piece of injera flatbread on a work surface.
Spread a portion of the cheese mixture onto the center of the injera, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Top the cheese with a dollop of beurre-monte and fold the edges of the injera over the filling to enclose.
